forecasts.reasoning: The time horizon is extremely short (only 39 days between the April 23 forecast date and the June 1 resolution date). Although the Zimbabwean government recently made its first cash payment of $3.1 million (1% of claims for 378 out of 740 approved farms) in late March/early April, typical sovereign disbursement schedules for cash-strapped nations operate on quarterly or longer cycles. The $10 million total budget allocated for 2025 means they have the funds to pay the remaining 362 approved farms, but there is no scheduled date for this second batch and stretching the remaining budgeted payments across the year is likely. Furthermore, recent reports (April 21, 2025) indicate that the official Commercial Farmers Union disputes the representativeness of the agreement, labeling the payouts as 'token gestures' to the most vulnerable. This organizational pushback could complicate or slow down the processing of further batches. Given the PR value of the first payment has just been capitalized on, a follow-up payment within 5 weeks is improbable.

The CDC currently has a Level 2 Travel Health Notice (THN) for Colombia due to a yellow fever outbreak, which was just updated on April 15, 2025. Evaluating the criteria for Level 3 (Reconsider Nonessential Travel), the CDC heavily reserves this level for severe, unmitigable threats to US travelers, such as Ebola or uncontrolled global pandemics (like COVID-19). Because yellow fever is entirely vaccine-preventable, US travelers can protect themselves completely prior to departure by getting vaccinated. Historically, even massive yellow fever or dengue outbreaks (like Brazil in 2018) remained at Level 2, as the core advice to travelers stands to practice enhanced precautions—namely, getting the shot and preventing mosquito bites. Given the CDC just reaffirmed the Level 2 status days ago, and there are currently very few if any Level 3/4 THNs active globally, the probability of the CDC escalating this to Level 3 or higher by June 1, 2025 is very low.

Dan Osborn actively established an exploratory committee for the 2026 US Senate race in Nebraska against incumbent Sen. Pete Ricketts on April 3, 2025. Following this, he has been aggressively releasing polling (tying him with Ricketts) and fundraising, positioning himself publicly for a race. Under FEC regulations, exploratory efforts that involve fundraising and polling often quickly trigger the $5,000 threshold that mandates formal registration and a candidacy announcement within a short window. A mid-April report noted a "FEC warning" regarding Osborn's activities, suggesting that his operational scale is forcing official candidate designation very soon. His rapid series of campaign-like actions (rallies, internal polling, broad fundraising appeals against a specific opponent) points to an imminent official announcement. The date June 1, 2025 is just under 6 weeks away. The combination of his established momentum and legal/FEC pressures to formally declare his candidacy points to a high likelihood of an announcement by June 1, 2025. Because some candidates artificially delay "official" announcements, my probability doesn't exceed 80%, but all signals are trending rapidly toward an impending declaration.

The current CDC Travel Health Notice for Yellow fever in Colombia is Level 2. The CDC reserves Level 3 ("Avoid Nonessential Travel") for extreme, unmitigable threats without widely available prevention measures (such as COVID-19 initially, or Marburg virus in Rwanda). Yellow Fever is a well-known disease with a highly effective, widely available single-dose vaccine that confers lifelong immunity. Because travelers can reliably protect themselves by getting vaccinated, the CDC is essentially unprecedented in issuing a Level 3 "Avoid Nonessential Travel" warning for a Yellow Fever outbreak. Massive historical outbreaks of Yellow Fever (like Brazil's in 2017-2018, with thousands of cases) only reached Level 2. Furthermore, even outbreaks of highly fatal diseases without vaccines (like Ebola in Uganda) have frequently remained at Level 2. Therefore, it is highly improbable that the CDC escalates Colombia's Yellow fever outbreak (currently 59 cases in Tolima) to a Level 3 notice before June 1, 2025. The probability is set at the functional floor.

The Zimbabwean government announced on April 9, 2025, that it made a $3.1 million cash disbursement to a 'first batch' of 378 farms out of a larger pool of 740 already-approved farms. The 2025 National Budget allocates $10 million for this compensation under the Global Compensation Deed, meaning sufficient funds appear to be set aside to cover the estimated ~$3 million needed for the remaining 362 approved farms. While the fact that the 740 farms are 'approved' and structured into 'batches' strongly implies further payouts are queued up, the timeframe to June 1, 2025 (less than 40 days from today) is quite tight. Historically, sovereign administrative processes in Zimbabwe face significant friction and delays (taking 5 years since the 2020 deal to make this very small first payment). In addition, on April 16, a major dissenting union representing other farmers explicitly rejected the compensation deal, which could stall momentum or pause the administrative rollout. Given the competing pressures of an already-approved pending tranche against typical bureaucratic delays and recent farmer backlash, I assign a 40% probability.

Dan Osborn formed an exploratory committee for a 2026 Senate run against Sen. Pete Ricketts on April 3, 2025. Only a week later, his committee released a poll showing him tied with Ricketts. This rapid succession of campaign-like activities strongly indicates he is moving quickly toward a formal run. While 2026 is still a long way off, exploratory committees often culminate in an official launch within 1 to 3 months, which overlaps closely with the June 1, 2025 deadline framework. Since the deadline is only slightly under two months from his initial exploration announcement, there is a risk he waits until summer or early fall (which would resolve NO). However, his immediate release of internal polling and active media statements makes an earlier, momentum-sustaining announcement before June 1 more likely than not.
